model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Docs link in the navigation

Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to find the "Docs" link in the navigation bar.
The red crosshair is currently at (308, 151) relative to some coordinate system (based on the watermark). No, wait, the watermark says (308, 151). Let's look at the image.
The crosshair is on the word "BUILD".
The navigation bar is at the top. The "Docs" link is in the middle of the navigation bar.
The current crosshair position is roughly at x=245, y=205 in terms of the image pixels.
Let's estimate the coordinates of "Docs".
The nav bar is at the top.
"Docs" is the third link from the left (after Home, Pricing).
Coordinates of "Docs":
x is around 300.
y is around 40.
Current crosshair: x ~ 245, y ~ 205.
Wait, let me re-examine the crosshair.
It's located at (x=245, y=205) in raw pixel coordinates of the 1000x500 image? No, the image is 1000x500.
Actually, looking at the image, the crosshair is centered around (245, 205).
The "Docs" link is at approximately (300, 40).
dx = 300 - 245 = 55
dy = 40 - 205 = -165
Let's refine the estimate.
Crosshair center: x=245, y=205.
"Docs" link center: x=300, y=40.
dx = 55, dy = -165.
Let's re-check the crosshair location.
The crosshair is on the 'U' of BUILD.
The 'Docs' link is to the right and way up.
Docs is at x=300, y=40.
Crosshair is at x=245, y=205.
Offset: dx = 55, dy = -165.
Actually, let me look at the image dimensions. It's 1000x500.
The crosshair is at (245, 205).
The 'Docs' text is roughly at (300, 40).
dx = 55, dy = -165.
Wait, let's look at the provided watermark. (308, 151).
If the crosshair is at (308, 151), then:
Docs is at x=300, y=40.
dx = 300 - 308 = -8
dy = 40 - 151 = -111.
Let's check where (308, 151) is.
x=308 is about 30% from the left.
y=151 is about 30% from the top.
In the image, the crosshair is indeed around (245, 205). The watermark might be misleading or from a different frame. I should trust the visual red crosshair.
Crosshair: x=245, y=205.
Docs center: x=300, y=40.
dx = 55, dy = -165.
Actually, let's look closer.
"Home" center ~ 145, 40
"Pricing" center ~ 225, 40
"Docs" center ~ 300, 40
"About" center ~ 370, 40
"Contact" center ~ 455, 40
Crosshair is at x=245, y=205.
dx = 300 - 245 = 55
dy = 40 - 205 = -165
Confidence: 0.9.
